coredata.dc:title: Facile synthetic Photoluminescent Graphene Quantum dots encapsulated β-cyclodextrin drug carrier system for the management of macular degeneration: Detailed analytical and biological investigations
coredata.prism:publicationName: Journal of Photochemistry and Photobiology B: Biology
coredata.dc:creator: Qian, Cheng
Yan, Panshi
Wan, Guangming
Liang, Shenzhi
Dong, Yi
Wang, Jiong
coredata.dc:description: Drug administration by effective nano-carriers is an emerging and growing technology in the field of bio-medicine and particularly Age –related macular degeneration (AMD). This developed nanomaterials based methods with drug administration maximizes the biocompatibility and systemically increases drug delivery profile for the drugs. Herein, we described the effective drug molecules delivery profiles by the hydrothermally synthesized graphene quantum dots (GQDs) encapsulated with supramolecular β-cyclodextrin (β-CD) as a drug delivery system for AMD. The drug release profiles were analysed and plotted by two different types of drugs ((Bevacizumab (Bev) and Ranibizumab (Ran))) and compounds displayed an initial burst delivery percentage of 55.7 ± 1.6% and 52.2 ± 2.6, respectively, within 15 min. After 1 h, 94.2% (Ran) and 93.1% (Bev) of loaded drug molecules were released from the β-CD encapsulated GQDs in sustained manner. The biocompatibility of the synthesized carriers was investigated quantitatively and qualitatively with the mouse Fibroblast L929 cell line. The biological cell analysis observed by calculated cell count and green fluorescence visualization has been clearly confirmed the samples are non-toxic and highly compatible to the cells with more than 90% cell viability after 5 days cell culture. The observed material properties and biological results demonstrated that the suitability of the developed nano-carriers for the drug delivery system in the AMD.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Graphene
Quantum dots
Macular degeneration
β-Cyclodextrin
Ranibizumab

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Synthesis of β-Cyclodextrin Encapsulated Graphene Quantum Dots
Drug Loading of β-Cyclodextrin Encapsulated GQDs

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Schematic representation of β-cyclodextrin encapsulated Graphene quantum dots.

A. FTIR spectra (β –cd (pink), β –cd/GQDs (blue)), B. XPS spectra and C. PL spectra of the β –cd/GQDs in different excitations. (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)
A. HR-TEM images of β –cd/GQDs (a&b) and particles size distributions of GQDs (c).
A. cell viability diagram with normal human fibroblast cell line, B. Cumulative drug release of β –cd/GQDs in different time intervals.
CLSM fluorescence images of fibroblast cells on the (a) GQDs, (b)β –cd and (c) β –cd/GQDs.

Investigation of anti-angiogenic activity by the tube formation treated with dual drug loaded β –cd and β –cd/GQDs components; (A) No. of tube formation treated with different combinations of compunds, (B) No. of tube formation treated with different concentrations of drug molecules on synthesized β –cd/GQDs.

head.ce:title: Facile synthetic Photoluminescent Graphene Quantum dots encapsulated β-cyclodextrin drug carrier system for the management of macular degeneration: Detailed analytical and biological investigations

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Ophthalmology, First Affiliated Hospital of Zhengzhou University, Henan Province Eye Hospital, Zhengzhou 450052, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: First Affiliated Hospital of Zhengzhou University

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Drug administration by effective nano-carriers is an emerging and growing technology in the field of bio-medicine and particularly Age –related macular degeneration (AMD). This developed nanomaterials based methods with drug administration maximizes the biocompatibility and systemically increases drug delivery profile for the drugs. Herein, we described the effective drug molecules delivery profiles by the hydrothermally synthesized graphene quantum dots (GQDs) encapsulated with supramolecular β-cyclodextrin (β-CD) as a drug delivery system for AMD. The drug release profiles were analysed and plotted by two different types of drugs ((Bevacizumab (Bev) and Ranibizumab (Ran))) and compounds displayed an initial burst delivery percentage of 55.7 ± 1.6% and 52.2 ± 2.6, respectively, within 15 min. After 1 h, 94.2% (Ran) and 93.1% (Bev) of loaded drug molecules were released from the β-CD encapsulated GQDs in sustained manner. The biocompatibility of the synthesized carriers was investigated quantitatively and qualitatively with the mouse Fibroblast L929 cell line. The biological cell analysis observed by calculated cell count and green fluorescence visualization has been clearly confirmed the samples are non-toxic and highly compatible to the cells with more than 90% cell viability after 5 days cell culture. The observed material properties and biological results demonstrated that the suitability of the developed nano-carriers for the drug delivery system in the AMD.

ce:section.ce:para: The macula is main part of the eye retina, highly responsible for the human vision and its affected by the different factors such as drusen formation, choroidal neovascularization and epithelium abnormality is called Age –related macular degeneration (AMD) [1–3]. The AMD disorder is a complex illness accounts for the 9% of blindness worldwide and its predictable to affect approximately 300 million peoples within 2040 [4,5]. In recent biomedical researchers of AMD are focused and developed the treatment strategies by the intravitreal therapy, which is suppressed blood vessels leakage to avoid the destroying blood vessels in AMD with the strategy to effective drug target to vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) [6–8]. The VEGF is powerful intermediary factor of angiogenesis and inflammatory reactions. Hence, the active targeting agents of VEGF expressions by the nanoformulations, which have the properties of anti-inflammatory and anti-permeability is deliberated as an antiangiogenic strategy [9].

In general quantum dots (QDs) have strong and stable luminescence properties owing to their quantum confinement effects [10–12]. The progress of bio compatible graphene QDs derivative of the carbon materials is essential for biomedical applications [13]. Graphene materials have great growing research attention in many fields due to its excellent abilities of conductivity of thermal of electrical areas, good electron mobility, and biocompatibility. So it has been applied efficiently in the applications of nano-electronics, bio-imaging [14] and chiefly drug delivery [15]. Recently, many bio-materials research have been attentive on the graphene quantum dots (GQDs), because of its outstanding properties such as photoluminescence, chemical inertness, discrete bandgap, high compatibility with cells. GQDs are less toxic and hydrophobic in nature and able to stable robust fluorescence properties as promising material for drug delivery [16]. Bestowing to recent studies on graphene based materials, especially graphene sheets in nano level, graphene quantum dots having lower toxicity and cell compatibility than bulk graphene due to their exclusive structural properties making them constructive and effective materials as targeted drug delivery agents [17]. Moreover, GQDs have great specific surface area comparable to the bulk counterparts and the presence of potential active functional groups has to be played important role in immobilization of drug molecules through electrostatically or covalent bonding.

Currently, the nanomaterials with supramolecular compounds have fascinated special interest in drug delivery by the formation non-covalent interactions via supramolecular chemistry. The supramolecular compounds have excellent drug delivery properties of high level mass transfer, structural control, high compatibility and degradation nature [18–20]. The β-cyclodextrins (β-CD) are significant supramolecular compound from the cyclodextrin family that having the great capability to capture hydrophobic molecular drugs with the support of hydrophilic outer surface to form macromolecular structures [21–24]. Due to its potential abilities of complexation with the hydrophobic drug molecules with low toxicity, good compatibility are denoted as a potential material for drug delivery system [25,26]. Owing to this unique structural nature, β-CDs have great promises of enhanced solubility to enable the favourable drug loading with controlled release with chemical stability of poorly soluble drugs [17,27,28]. Herein, we described ranibizumab and bevacizumab loaded (β-CD)/GQDs as a targeted drug delivery system for AMD therapy.

The commercial graphene oxide powder was purchased from Alfa Aesar Company. The other chemicals such as sodium nitrate, potassium permanganate with analytical grade and 98% Sulphuric acid, nitric acid 30% hydrogen peroxide were obtained from Shanghai Chemical Reagents Company, China. The β-cyclodextrin powder was acquired from Guangdong Guanghua Chemical Reagent Co.Ltd., China, which were used. All the chemical compounds and reagents were used without further purification as analytical grades.

Graphene quantum dots were synthesized from graphene oxide thermal exfoliation by using concentrated nitric oxide as per described in literature [29]. Briefly, powder of graphene oxide (0.5 g) was taken into a quartz tube by sealed one end. The argon gas was inserted for 10 s through other end by using rubber stopper and then quickly kept into a tube furnace for 1 min at 1000 °C. Followed by the powder was refluxed in nitric acid (6 M) for 1 day. The refluxed solution was taken out for cooling and then centrifuged at 4500 rpm for 15 min. The separated supernatant was heated to evaporate water and acid residues at 200 °C. Finally, the brownish-red precipitate was obtained and subsequently dispersed in ultrapure water to remove ions and impurities by dialysis process.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis of β-Cyclodextrin Encapsulated Graphene Quantum Dots
ce:section.ce:para: As-synthesized GQDs are dispersed in PBS solution was allowable to make reaction with EDC and NHS for 20 min under stirring at room temperature. For synthesis of β-CD encapsulated GQDs, the synthesized GQDs were taken in a ratio of 1.0:0.1 (β-CD; GQDs) with the aqueous solution of β-cyclodextrin and then the mixture has been stirred energetically with PEG (1 g) solution for 30 min at 70 °C. As a final point, β-CD encapsulated on PEGylated GQDs were separated using centrifugation and washed thrice with distilled water and ethanol several times and afterward dehydrated in a vacuum air oven at 50 °C for 12 h. The probable schematic of present work has been shown in Fig. 1 .

Synthesized GQDs and bio composite material were chemically characterised by the combination of Fourier –transformer infra-red spectroscopy (FTIR), Transmission electron microscopy (TEM), Dynamic Light Scattering (DLS), Photo luminescence spectroscopy (PL) and Ultra violet- visible spectroscopy (UV–visible). FT-IR spectra of β-cyclodextrin and GQDs -β-cyclodextrin were recorded using Thermo Nicolet, Avatar 370. Size and morphology of GQDs was investigated by TEM- Hitachi H-800 electron microscope at voltage of 200 kV. UV–visible spectra of GQDs was characterised by Varian, Cary 5000. Photoluminescence spectra of synthesized GQDs was recorded by Hitachi 7000 fluorescence spectrophotometer. DLS spectrum was recorded by Zeta sizer Nano ZS.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Drug Loading of β-Cyclodextrin Encapsulated GQDs
ce:section.ce:para: Drug loading onto β-cyclodextrin encapsulated quantum dots by a facile strategy. Briefly, PEG functionalised GQDs 2 mL and 2 mg of drugs ((bevacizumab (bev) and ranibizumab (Ran))) in 2 mL acetone solution were emulsified with support of 1% PVA by using ultrasonication for 5 min. The resulting suspension was then maintained under stirring for 24 h. The obtained suspension was centrifuged for remove unbound drug molecules from the β-CD/GQDs at 7300 rpm for 1 h and then was re-dispersed in water. This centrifugation process was repetitive for three times to eliminate all of the unbound drug molecules. The drug molecules loaded β-CD-GQD was freeze-dried and kept in a desiccator.

Graphene quantum dots were synthesized by numerous methods from a various carbon -based materials through thermally organized oxidation or reduction mechanisms. Photoluminescence GQDs, which has been exhibited various morphological features and optical properties depending on its particle size and the chemical groups at the edge. In this present wok, we were synthesized spherical GQDs from the method of thermally exfoliation and refluxing of graphite oxide under concentrated nitric acid and its properties are confirmed by the techniques of FTIR,TEM, DLS, PL and UV–visible spectroscopy. Fig. 2(A) shows the FT-IR spectra of GQDs having many chemically active functional groups, including hydroxyl, carbonyl, and epoxy/ether groups. Generally, GQDs exhibited enhanced solubility, chemical stability with tremendous fluorescence properties owing to the existence of such a variety of functional group. FTIR spectrum of GQDs the broad peak at 3445.5 cm−1 attributed to OH group, small peak at 1375 cm−1 which is accredited to the carbonyl (CO) stretching vibration confirming the occurrence of carboxylic functional groups. The important peaks at the range of 1630 cm−1 to 1650 cm−1 show the presence of CC bond which remained after the oxidation process.

The synthesized GQDs were also examined by PL spectra for investigating consistency of the QDs particle size distribution and fluorescence characteristics as displayed in Fig. 2(C). The maximum intensity for emission occurs at 567 nm. Additionally, to explore its photoluminescence properties of PEGylated GQDs, a thorough study had been analysed by exciting the GQD diffusions at the wavelength range from 450 to 700 nm. Their maximum photoluminescence emission intensity is observed at an excitation wavelength 567 nm and these excellent properties signified main advantages for these materials, permitting their efficient pursuing in model human cell lines in vitro. The chemical composition of graphene QDs was investigated and confirmed by the XPS analysis. The XPS overview range of graphene QDs displayed in Fig. 2(B). XPS analysis exhibited that C 1 s spectrum of GQDs, which confirms functional groups on the GQDs surface, amongst which a key peak at 285.4 eV was predicted to the CC bonding with sp2 orbital. Mainly, C 1 s XPS peaks were focused the binding energies 286.7, 288, 288.5, and 289.9 eV were corresponded to the sp3 hybridized carbons such as COC, CO, and COH, respectively.

Mostly, the size and morphology of nanoparticles have been highly involved and important factor as drug carrier prominently influence the cell uptake in the biological systems. Moreover, small size nanoparticle like quantum dots have the great prospective for transporting through various biological barriers and effortlessly permit for access into human cells and countless cellular compartments, encompassing the nucleus. The synthesized GQDs surface morphology was examined by TEM (Fig. 3a-b). The characteristic TEM images of GQDs showed equally distribution of nanoparticles with spherical shape as well as cubic like structures and diameter less than 5 nm, respectively. Additionally, the ultra-small sizes of the GQDs are confirmed by the particles size distribution analysis (Fig. 3c), which exhibited maximum particles size in the range of 2 nm.

The drug release profiles (Fig. 4B) were analysed and plotted by two different types of drugs (acetamidophenol (AC; hydrophilic) and etoricoxib (ET; hydrophobic)) loaded β-CD/GQDs drug careers displayed controlled release of drugs for the arthritis pain management. The drug molecules of AC- and ET- loaded β-CD/GQDs carrier compounds displayed an initial burst delivery percentage of 55.7 ± 1.6% and 52.2 ± 2.6, respectively, within 15 min. After 1 h, 94.2% (AC) and 93.1% (ET) of loaded drug molecules were released from the β-CD encapsulated GQDs in sustained manner. The drug release profiles of the prepared nano-carrier compounds with β-CD encapsulation exhibited that the maximum percentage of cumulative drug release profile comparable to the GQDs in the absence of β-CD molecules. And additionally, the drug release rate of the compound was triggered at the pH 5.5 compared to the pH 7.4. Since maximum of the AC drug molecules dispersed out after 1 h time, the triggered mechanism was not observed it may be due to its hydrophilic behaviour with that of the β-CD carrier.

The biocompatibility of the synthesized GQDs in the presence and absence of β-CD was investigated quantitatively and qualitatively with the Mouse Fibroblast L929 cell line by using MTT assay. The quantitative analysis of the cell viability in the presence of samples has been observed in the absorbance at 570 nm. The L929 cell culture from the β-CD/GQDs was calculated the cell viability after 1, 3 and 5 days and it results clearly confirmed that samples are non-toxic and highly compatible to the cells with more than 90% cell viablity (Fig. 4A). The qualitative analysis of the cell compatibility was visualized by the fluorescence microscopic technique by based on the cell attachment and cell growth on the synthesized samples with staining as shown in Fig. 5 . The fibroblast cell growth and elongation on the samples was confirmed the samples suitability and rate of viability. The green fluorescence images of cell growth were captured with Fluorescein diacetate (FDA) dye staining at the different time intervals. The living fibroblast cell membranes have the ability hydrolysed with fluorescent compounds and passed through its membranes to given green fluorescence emission. Hence, we can clearly observe the surviving cells and growth of the fibroblast cells by the green fluorescence morphology of the cells.

The anti-angiogenic efficiency of drug molecules with synthesized β-CD/GQDs was investigated by using the method of Matrigel-based assay (Fig. 6 ). The effective anti-angiogenic activity of drug carrier was tested through the tube-forming capability and calculating the values of of totally connected tubes. The number of tube formation of β-CD and GQDs/β-CD nanoformulations has been displayed active inhibition comparable to the bare drug molecules and VEGF (Fig. 6A). In particularly, the tube formation inhibition of drug loaded GQDs/β-CD nanoformulations has exhibited outstanding activity compared to the drug loaded β-CD molecules. It may be the effective drug caring property and bioactivity of GQDs on the tube formations. In some reported investigations, researchers clearly demonstrated that VEGF function has been inhibited by the engineered nanoparticles, because of VEGF-A function might be abridged with the bonding of nanoparticles. The ultra-small GQDs material can be given excellent activity for the inhibition of tube formation in the AMD therapy. The anti-angiogenic abilities of synthesized GQDs/β-CD nano-carriers have been studied with the different concentrations of dual drug molecules, which exhibited that increasing concentrations of GQDs and drug have increasing inhibitory effect of tube formation in the analyses as shown in Fig. 6B.

Overall, the investigated data and results demonstrated that supramolecular and biologically active β-CD encapsulated GQDs nano-carriers are a promising drug delivery platform for the effective loading capacity and standard delivery profiles for arthritis pain management comparable to the GQDs in the absence of β-CD molecules. The microscopic analyses of synthesized samples exhibited uniformly distributed nanoparticles with spherical shape as well as cubic like structures and diameter less than 5 nm respectively. The drug release profiles were analysed and plotted by hydrophilic and hydrophobic nature drugs and compounds displayed excellent drug release profile (~94%) from the β-CD encapsulated GQDs in sustained manner. The biological cell analysis (Mouse Fibroblast L929 cell line) observed by calculated cell count and green fluorescence visualization has been clearly confirmed the samples are highly compatible to the cells with more than 90% cell viability. Therefore, the drug loaded β-CD encapsulated GQDs nano-carriers could be a probable materials for the effective drug delivery system for arthritis pain management.
